title.en: IEEE 802 Overview and Architecture: The Foundation Map (Clauses 1, 3-7)
title.zh: IEEE 802 概览与架构：体系地基全景（第 1、3-7 章）

blocks.en: IEEE Std 802-2024 is the keystone document of the whole IEEE 802 family. It does not specify any single network technology; instead it sets the shared vocabulary, the layered reference model (RM), and the baseline requirements that every member standard (Wi-Fi, Ethernet, and the rest) inherits. Clause 1.1 (Scope) says this standard describes the published IEEE 802 standards for frame-based data networks plus a reference model for protocols, and includes a way to identify public, private, and standard protocols. Clause 1.2 (Purpose) states the document is the foundation for the IEEE 802 family covering local area networks (LANs), metropolitan area networks (MANs), personal area networks (PANs), and regional area networks (RANs).
blocks.zh: IEEE Std 802-2024 是整个 IEEE 802 标准家族的基石文档。它本身不规定任何单一的网络技术，而是确立所有成员标准（Wi-Fi、以太网等）共同继承的术语、分层参考模型（RM, reference model）以及基线要求。第 1.1 章（范围）指出，本标准描述了 IEEE 发布的、面向基于帧的数据网络的各项 IEEE 802 标准，并给出一套协议参考模型，同时包含一种识别公有、私有和标准协议的方法。第 1.2 章（目的）说明本文档是 IEEE 802 家族的基础，覆盖局域网（LAN）、城域网（MAN）、个域网（PAN）和区域网（RAN）。

blocks.en: Clause 1.3 (Word usage) fixes the meaning of four words you will meet everywhere in IEEE 802: shall = a mandatory requirement; should = recommended but not required; may = permitted (a choice); can = able to (a statement of capability). Reading 'shall' vs 'should' correctly is the difference between conformance and a nice-to-have.
blocks.zh: 第 1.3 章（用词约定）固定了四个在 IEEE 802 中随处可见的词的含义：shall = 强制性要求；should = 推荐但非强制；may = 允许（一种可选项）；can = 能够（对能力的陈述）。正确区分 shall 与 should，决定了某条款是合规必需项还是锦上添花项。

blocks.en: Clause 3 builds the shared dictionary. Clause 3.1 (Definitions) pins down the core nouns: an end station is a source or destination of link-layer traffic; a bridge interconnects two or more bridgeable IEEE 802 networks at Layer 2 (it forwards and filters MAC frames); a station is simply 'an end station or bridge'; an access domain is a set of stations sharing one medium-access-control (MAC) protocol on a common channel. It also defines protocol categories used in protocol identification: a public protocol is published but controlled by a non-standards organization, a private protocol is controlled by a private organization, and a standard protocol is published and controlled by a standards body. Clause 3.2 (Acronyms and abbreviations) is the master expansion list, e.g. LLC = logical link control, SAP = service access point, MSDU = MAC service data unit, EUI = Extended Unique Identifier, SLAP = Structured Local Address Plan.
blocks.zh: 第 3 章构建共享词典。第 3.1 章（定义）锁定核心名词：端站（end station）是链路层流量的源或目的；网桥（bridge）在二层互连两个或多个可桥接的 IEEE 802 网络（它转发并过滤 MAC 帧）；站（station）就是“端站或网桥”；接入域（access domain）是共享同一介质访问控制（MAC）协议、在同一公共信道上的一组站。它还定义了用于协议识别的协议类别：公有协议（public protocol）已公开但由非标准化组织控制，私有协议（private protocol）由私营组织控制，标准协议（standard protocol）已公开且由标准化机构控制。第 3.2 章（缩略语）是缩写总表，例如 LLC = 逻辑链路控制，SAP = 服务接入点，MSDU = MAC 服务数据单元，EUI = 扩展唯一标识符，SLAP = 结构化本地地址规划。

blocks.en: Clause 4 zooms out to the whole family. Clause 4.1 (Key concepts) explains that IEEE 802 is not limited to the physical layer (PHY) and data link layer (DLL): these networks use frame-based, addressed communication over many media. Early LANs were shared-medium broadcast; bridges (Clause 5.3.2) later let networks scale far beyond one shared medium while still looking like simple peer-to-peer links to end stations, and point-to-point full-duplex links emerged. It also introduces PANs, body area networks (BANs), and the wireless variants (WLAN, WPAN, WRAN). Clause 4.2 (Application and support) gives the intended uses (file transfer, multimedia, process control, IoT devices, and so on) as illustrative, not mandatory, lists. Clause 4.3 (An international family of standards) notes that several IEEE 802 standards have been adopted by ISO/IEC JTC 1, ITU-T, and ITU-R, making them international standards. Clause 4.4 (IEEE 802 standards) lists the current members (Figure 1) and explains that approved amendments become part of the base standard and are folded in at the next revision (Figure 2).
blocks.zh: 第 4 章放大到整个家族。第 4.1 章（关键概念）说明 IEEE 802 并不局限于物理层（PHY）和数据链路层（DLL）：这些网络在多种介质上使用基于帧、带地址的通信。早期 LAN 是共享介质广播；后来网桥（第 5.3.2 章）让网络的规模远超单一共享介质，同时在端站看来仍像简单的对等链路，并出现了点到点全双工链路。它还引入了 PAN、体域网（BAN）以及无线变体（WLAN、WPAN、WRAN）。第 4.2 章（应用与支持）以举例方式（而非强制清单）列出预期用途，如文件传输、多媒体、过程控制、物联网设备等。第 4.3 章（国际标准家族）指出若干 IEEE 802 标准已被 ISO/IEC JTC 1、ITU-T 和 ITU-R 采纳，成为国际标准。第 4.4 章（IEEE 802 标准）列出当前成员（图 1），并说明已批准的修正案会成为基础标准的一部分，并在下次修订时并入（图 2）。

blocks.en: Clause 5 is the architectural heart. Clause 5.1 (Introduction) states the IEEE 802 RM is derived from the OSI basic reference model (OSI/RM) and emphasizes the lowest two OSI layers, the PHY and the DLL. The DLL is split into two sublayers: the logical link control (LLC) sublayer on top, running over the MAC sublayer. More detailed per-standard RMs live in Annex B; implementation models (IMs) are even more specific.
blocks.zh: 第 5 章是架构核心。第 5.1 章（引言）指出 IEEE 802 RM 派生自 OSI 基本参考模型（OSI/RM），并聚焦 OSI 最底两层——PHY 与 DLL。DLL 拆分为两个子层：上方的逻辑链路控制（LLC）子层运行于 MAC 子层之上。各标准更详细的 RM 见附录 B；实现模型（IM, implementation model）则更为具体。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 220" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="40" y="10" width="280" height="30"/><text x="180" y="30" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Upper layer protocols</text><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="320" y2="55" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="330" y="58" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="start">LSAP</text><rect x="40" y="60" width="280" height="30"/><text x="180" y="80" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">LLC sublayer</text><line x1="40" y1="105" x2="320" y2="105" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="330" y="108" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="start">MSAP</text><rect x="40" y="110" width="280" height="30"/><text x="180" y="130" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">MAC sublayer</text><line x1="40" y1="155" x2="320" y2="155" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="330" y="158" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="start">PSAP</text><rect x="40" y="160" width="280" height="30"/><text x="180" y="180" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">PHY</text><text x="180" y="212" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Medium</text><text x="10" y="80" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" transform="rotate(-90 10 80)">DLL</text></svg>

caption.en: The IEEE 802 reference model for end stations (Clause 5.2, Figure 3): the DLL splits into LLC over MAC, each boundary exposing a service access point (SAP) — LSAP above LLC, MSAP between LLC and MAC, PSAP between MAC and PHY.
caption.zh: 面向端站的 IEEE 802 参考模型（第 5.2 章，图 3）：DLL 拆为 LLC 叠加于 MAC 之上，每个边界暴露一个服务接入点（SAP）——LLC 之上为 LSAP，LLC 与 MAC 之间为 MSAP，MAC 与 PHY 之间为 PSAP。

blocks.en: Clause 5.2 walks the end-station model layer by layer. Clause 5.2.1 (SAPs) names the interface ports: link service access points (LSAPs) above the LLC, MAC service access points (MSAPs) between LLC and MAC, and the PHY service access point (PSAP); optional media-independent control SAPs (MICSAP / MICLSAP / MICPSAP) connect a parallel control plane. Clause 5.2.2 (LLC sublayer) sits between the MAC sublayer and the network layer; since 2014 it also encompasses IEEE 802.1AE MAC security, 802.1AX link aggregation, and 802.1X authentication. Clause 5.2.3 (MAC sublayer) provides the data-transfer service whose unit is the MAC frame; its principal functions are frame delimiting and recognition, source/destination addressing (individual and group), transparent data transfer, error protection via a frame check sequence, and arbitrating access to the medium. Clause 5.2.4 (PHY) transmits and receives modulated signals, mapping bit blocks to symbols. Clause 5.2.5 (Layer and sublayer management) notes that the LLC, MAC, and PHY each include a management component exposing managed objects (detailed in Clause 7).
blocks.zh: 第 5.2 章逐层走读端站模型。第 5.2.1 章（SAP）命名各接口端口：LLC 之上的链路服务接入点（LSAP）、LLC 与 MAC 之间的 MAC 服务接入点（MSAP）以及 PHY 服务接入点（PSAP）；可选的介质无关控制 SAP（MICSAP / MICLSAP / MICPSAP）连接一个并行控制平面。第 5.2.2 章（LLC 子层）位于 MAC 子层与网络层之间；自 2014 年起还涵盖 IEEE 802.1AE MAC 安全、802.1AX 链路聚合与 802.1X 认证。第 5.2.3 章（MAC 子层）提供数据传输服务，其单元为 MAC 帧；主要功能包括帧定界与识别、源/目的寻址（单个与组）、透明数据传输、通过帧校验序列进行差错防护，以及对介质访问的仲裁。第 5.2.4 章（PHY）发送和接收调制信号，将比特块映射为符号。第 5.2.5 章（层与子层管理）指出 LLC、MAC、PHY 各自含一个管理组件，暴露被管对象（详见第 7 章）。

blocks.en:
flowchart LR
  A[End station] -->|repeater 5.3.1| B[Same access domain]
  A -->|bridge 5.3.2 Layer 2| C[Other IEEE 802 network]
  A -->|router 5.3.3 Layer 3| D[Other network type]
blocks.zh:
flowchart LR
  A[端站] -->|中继器 5.3.1| B[同一接入域]
  A -->|网桥 5.3.2 二层| C[其他 IEEE 802 网络]
  A -->|路由器 5.3.3 三层| D[其他类型网络]

blocks.en: Clause 5.3 (Interconnection and interworking) classifies interconnection devices by the layer they operate at — the three-way split shown above. Clause 5.3.1 (Interconnection at the PHY) covers repeaters, which extend a single access domain at the physical layer (only one station transmits at a time). Clause 5.3.2 (MAC-sublayer interconnection: Bridges) covers bridges, which relay and filter MAC frames entirely within the MAC sublayer using IEEE 802.1Q, and are transparent to LLC and higher layers; this is where VLANs, provider bridging, time-sensitive networking (TSN), and data-center bridging live. (Note: the standard avoids the word 'switch' for these functions.) Clause 5.3.3 (Network-layer interconnection: Routers) covers routers, which forward on Layer 3 (network-layer) addresses; routers operate as IEEE 802 end stations, but their internal specification lies outside IEEE 802 — for example IP routers carry much of the actual user traffic.
blocks.zh: 第 5.3 章（互连与互通）按设备工作的层次对互连设备分类——即上图的三分法。第 5.3.1 章（PHY 处的互连）讲中继器（repeater），它在物理层扩展单一接入域（同一时刻只有一个站发送）。第 5.3.2 章（MAC 子层互连：网桥）讲网桥，它完全在 MAC 子层内、依据 IEEE 802.1Q 转发并过滤 MAC 帧，对 LLC 及更高层透明；VLAN、运营商桥接、时间敏感网络（TSN）与数据中心桥接都在此。（注意：标准刻意不用“交换机”一词来指这些功能。）第 5.3.3 章（网络层互连：路由器）讲路由器，它依据三层（网络层）地址转发；路由器作为 IEEE 802 端站运行，但其内部规范不在 IEEE 802 范围内——例如 IP 路由器承载了大量实际用户流量。

blocks.en: Clause 6 sets the floor every IEEE 802 network must clear. Clause 6.1 (Services supported) says a bridgeable IEEE 802 network must, at minimum, support the MAC Internal Sublayer Service of IEEE Std 802.1AC, delivering LLC data from one MSAP to n other MSAPs. Clause 6.2 (Error ratios) gives the conformance numbers: for wired/optical media within an access domain, an undelivered MAC frame due only to the PHY shall be less than 8 x 10^-8 per octet; for wired media with frames shorter than 2048 octets, an undetected MSDU error shall be less than 5 x 10^-14 per octet; for wireless media the error performance is variable over time (no fixed bound).
blocks.zh: 第 6 章设定每个 IEEE 802 网络必须达到的下限。第 6.1 章（支持的服务）规定：可桥接的 IEEE 802 网络至少必须支持 IEEE Std 802.1AC 的 MAC 内部子层服务，将 LLC 数据从一个 MSAP 投递到另外 n 个 MSAP。第 6.2 章（差错率）给出合规数值：对接入域内的有线/光纤介质，仅因 PHY 导致未正确投递的 MAC 帧概率 shall 小于每字节 8 × 10⁻⁸；对帧短于 2048 字节的有线介质，MSDU 出现未检出差错的概率 shall 小于每字节 5 × 10⁻¹⁴；对无线介质，差错性能随时间变化（无固定边界）。

blocks.en: Clause 7 covers network management. Clause 7.1 (General) splits management into two broad kinds: general-purpose (monitor/control for many purposes) and special-purpose (aimed at one specific aspect). Clause 7.2 introduces general-purpose management; Clause 7.2.1 (Management functions) lists the five classic functional areas — configuration, fault, performance, security, and accounting management (the FCAPS set). Clause 7.2.2 (Management architecture) is built on managed objects that model management semantics, operable locally or remotely via a general-purpose protocol such as Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) or Network Configuration Protocol (NETCONF). Clause 7.2.3 (Managed object definitions) says each managed object needs a functionality definition, a syntax for operations, and an address — an object identifier (OID, Clause 10) or a Uniform Resource Name (URN, Clause 11) — and these are modeled with SMIv2 (for SNMP MIBs) or YANG. Clause 7.3 (Special-purpose IEEE 802 network management standards) covers protocols used when a general-purpose one is inappropriate: Connectivity Fault Management (802.1Q), the Operations, Administration, and Maintenance (OAM) protocol (802.3), and the Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP, 802.1AB).
blocks.zh: 第 7 章讲网络管理。第 7.1 章（总则）将管理分为两大类：通用型（面向多种目的的监控/控制）与专用型（针对某一特定方面）。第 7.2 章引入通用管理；第 7.2.1 章（管理功能）列出五大经典功能域——配置、故障、性能、安全与计费管理（即 FCAPS）。第 7.2.2 章（管理架构）建立在被管对象（managed object）之上，用以建模管理语义，可本地操作或经通用协议远程操作，如简单网络管理协议（SNMP）或网络配置协议（NETCONF）。第 7.2.3 章（被管对象定义）规定每个被管对象需要功能定义、操作语法以及地址——对象标识符（OID，第 10 章）或统一资源名（URN，第 11 章）——并用 SMIv2（用于 SNMP MIB）或 YANG 建模。第 7.3 章（专用 IEEE 802 网络管理标准）涵盖在通用协议不适用时所用的协议：连通性故障管理（802.1Q）、运维管理（OAM）协议（802.3）以及链路层发现协议（LLDP，802.1AB）。

summary.en: Coverage map: every clause in this lesson, by number
summary.zh: 覆盖地图：本课每个条款逐一对应

blocks.en: Clause 1: 1.1 Scope (describes the IEEE 802 standards + protocol RM + protocol identification); 1.2 Purpose (foundation for LAN/MAN/PAN/RAN family); 1.3 Word usage (shall/should/may/can). Clause 3: 3.1 Definitions (end station, bridge, station, access domain, public/private/standard protocol, EUI, SLAP, NUI); 3.2 Acronyms and abbreviations (LLC, SAP, MSDU, MAC, EUI, etc.). Clause 4: 4.1 Key concepts (frame-based addressed networks beyond PHY+DLL; shared medium to bridged to full-duplex; PAN/BAN/WLAN); 4.2 Application and support (illustrative use/device lists); 4.3 An international family of standards (ISO/IEC JTC 1, ITU-T, ITU-R adoption); 4.4 IEEE 802 standards (Figure 1 family; amendments fold into base, Figure 2). Clause 5: 5.1 Introduction (RM derived from OSI/RM; PHY + DLL; DLL = LLC over MAC); 5.2.1 SAPs (LSAP/MSAP/PSAP + optional MIC SAPs); 5.2.2 LLC sublayer (incl. 802.1AE/AX/X since 2014); 5.2.3 MAC sublayer (MAC frame; delimiting, addressing, transfer, FCS, medium access); 5.2.4 PHY (modulated signals, bits-to-symbols); 5.2.5 Layer and sublayer management (managed objects per sublayer); 5.3.1 Interconnection at the PHY (repeaters, one access domain); 5.3.2 Bridges (802.1Q relay/filter within MAC; transparent to LLC); 5.3.3 Routers (Layer 3 forwarding; spec outside 802). Clause 6: 6.1 Services supported (min: MAC Internal Sublayer Service of 802.1AC, one MSAP to n MSAPs); 6.2 Error ratios (< 8e-8/octet PHY loss wired/optical; < 5e-14/octet undetected MSDU error <2048 octets; wireless variable). Clause 7: 7.1 General (general-purpose vs special-purpose); 7.2.1 Management functions (configuration/fault/performance/security/accounting = FCAPS); 7.2.2 Management architecture (managed objects, local/remote, SNMP/NETCONF); 7.2.3 Managed object definitions (functionality + syntax + address via OID or URN; SMIv2/YANG); 7.3 Special-purpose standards (CFM in 802.1Q, OAM in 802.3, LLDP in 802.1AB).
blocks.zh: 第 1 章：1.1 范围（描述 IEEE 802 标准 + 协议 RM + 协议识别）；1.2 目的（LAN/MAN/PAN/RAN 家族的基础）；1.3 用词（shall/should/may/can）。第 3 章：3.1 定义（端站、网桥、站、接入域、公有/私有/标准协议、EUI、SLAP、NUI）；3.2 缩略语（LLC、SAP、MSDU、MAC、EUI 等）。第 4 章：4.1 关键概念（超越 PHY+DLL 的基于帧、带地址网络；共享介质→桥接→全双工；PAN/BAN/WLAN）；4.2 应用与支持（用途/设备的示例清单）；4.3 国际标准家族（被 ISO/IEC JTC 1、ITU-T、ITU-R 采纳）；4.4 IEEE 802 标准（图 1 家族；修正案并入基础标准，图 2）。第 5 章：5.1 引言（RM 派生自 OSI/RM；PHY + DLL；DLL = LLC 叠加 MAC）；5.2.1 SAP（LSAP/MSAP/PSAP + 可选 MIC SAP）；5.2.2 LLC 子层（自 2014 含 802.1AE/AX/X）；5.2.3 MAC 子层（MAC 帧；定界、寻址、传输、FCS、介质访问）；5.2.4 PHY（调制信号、比特转符号）；5.2.5 层与子层管理（各子层的被管对象）；5.3.1 PHY 处互连（中继器，单一接入域）；5.3.2 网桥（802.1Q 在 MAC 内转发/过滤；对 LLC 透明）；5.3.3 路由器（三层转发；规范不在 802 范围）。第 6 章：6.1 支持的服务（最低：802.1AC 的 MAC 内部子层服务，一个 MSAP 到 n 个 MSAP）；6.2 差错率（有线/光纤 PHY 丢帧 < 每字节 8e-8；<2048 字节有线介质未检出 MSDU 差错 < 每字节 5e-14；无线随时间变化）。第 7 章：7.1 总则（通用型与专用型）；7.2.1 管理功能（配置/故障/性能/安全/计费 = FCAPS）；7.2.2 管理架构（被管对象、本地/远程、SNMP/NETCONF）；7.2.3 被管对象定义（功能 + 语法 + 地址，经 OID 或 URN；SMIv2/YANG）；7.3 专用标准（802.1Q 的 CFM、802.3 的 OAM、802.1AB 的 LLDP）。

cites.note: Clause 1 Overview (Scope, Purpose, Word usage) and Clause 3 Definitions and acronyms.
Clause 4 Family of IEEE 802 standards (Key concepts, Application and support, international family, IEEE 802 standards / Figures 1-2).
Clause 5 Reference models: Introduction, end-station RM (SAPs, LLC, MAC, PHY, management), and interconnection (repeaters, bridges, routers).
Clause 6 General requirements (services supported, error ratios) and Clause 7 network management (general, functions, architecture, managed objects, special-purpose).
